studies.title: DiGA Cara Care für Reizdarm - Evaluation of a non-invasive, non-additionally burdensome, prospective, multicentered, two-armed, randomized trial with parallel group design
studies.brief_summary:
The digital health application Cara Care für Reizdarm (Cara Care for irritable bowel syndrome) is an app intended for the digital treatment of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). The app has the goal to reduce the severity of IBS symptoms and improve quality of life (QoL). In 2020, the efficacy of the app was already analysed with the help of standardized medical questionnaires.    
The planned clinical study has the aim to analyse these effects in more detail.

It will be evaluated whether the use of the app Cara Care für Reizdarm can lead to positive care effects for patients with IBS.

studies.title: Cara Care for Reflux and Functional Dyspepsia - Evaluation of positive effects for patients with gastroesophageal reflux disease in a non-invasive, prospective, two-arm, randomized study with parallel group design - CARE-R study
studies.brief_summary:
Gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) is a chronic disease of the oesophagus. With a prevalence of around 15-25% in the western world, it is one of the most common gastroenterological diseases. In Germany, one in five people suffer from reflux symptoms. 
The main symptoms of GERD include heartburn, acid regurgitation and regurgitation of stomach contents. However, it can also be accompanied by other non-specific symptoms such as epigastric and/or retrosternal pain, belching, nausea, sleep disturbances, a lumpy feeling in the throat, increased saliva secretion and respiratory symptoms such as hoarseness or a dry cough.

The aim of the planned scientific study is to examine whether positive effects (pVE) can be demonstrated in users of the app Cara Care for Reflux and Functional Dyspepsia compared to patients who do not use the app.

studies.title: Cara Care for Reflux and Functional Dyspepsia - Evaluation of positive effects for patients with functional dyspepsia in a non-invasive, prospective, two-arm, randomized study with parallel group design - CARE-D study
studies.brief_summary:
Functional dyspepsia (FD), also known as irritable stomach syndrome in Germany, comes with a multifaceted disease pattern. Around 10% of the population suffer from this chronic disease. Functional dyspepsia is relatively frequently associated with psychological diagnoses such as anxiety disorders, depression and somatization disorders. In addition, patients often suffer from sleep disorders, which worsen their quality of life and are associated with higher symptom severity. 
Typical symptoms of functional dyspepsia are epigastric pain and burning, postprandial fullness, nausea and early satiety. 

The aim of the planned scientific study is to examine whether positive care effects can be demonstrated in users of the app Cara Care for Reflux and Functional Dyspepsia compared to patients who do not use the app.
